# Rare Word Replacements — owner review file

*Generated by `scripts/19_select_rare_word_replacements.py`.*

One auto-selected replacement per rare-word verse. Review workflow: edit the replacement word in the header or the NEW line where the choice is wrong; DELETE an entire entry (header through NEW line) to leave that verse unchanged. Entries tagged `NOT in KJV`, `grammar MISMATCH`, or `NO-REPLACEMENT-FOUND` need the closest attention. A follow-up script will commit surviving entries to the database.

## abasing → lowering — II Corinthians 11:7
- source: User selected - the reason is when someone is in the presence of royality, they lower themselves to show respect.
- OLD: Have I committed an offence in abasing myself that ye might be exalted, because I have preached to you the gospel of God freely?
- NEW: Have I committed an offence in lowering myself that ye might be exalted, because I have preached to you the gospel of God freely?
- 

## abhorrest → hatest — Isaiah 7:16
- source: King James agent selection
- OLD: For before the child shall know to refuse the evil, and choose the good, the land that thou abhorrest shall be forsaken of both her kings.
- NEW: For before the child shall know to refuse the evil, and choose the good, the land that thou hatest shall be forsaken of both her kings.

## abhorrest → hatest — Romans 2:22
- source: Owner ruling 2026-07-16 — prior "abhor" both re-inflected the flagged word and broke the -est inflection; "hatest" matches the audited Isaiah 7:16 rendering of abhorrest and is common KJV vocabulary (Psalm 5:5). Agent alternative: "detestest" (rejected — 0 KJV uses, would itself be rare).
- OLD: Thou that sayest a man should not commit adultery, dost thou commit adultery? thou that abhorrest idols, dost thou commit sacrilege?
- NEW: Thou that sayest a man should not commit adultery, dost thou commit adultery? thou that hatest idols, dost thou commit sacrilege?

## abhorring → abomination — Isaiah 66:24
- source: King James agent selection
- OLD: And they shall go forth, and look upon the carcases of the men that have transgressed against me: for their worm shall not die, neither shall their fire be quenched; and they shall be an abhorring unto all flesh.  
- NEW: And they shall go forth, and look upon the carcases of the men that have transgressed against me: for their worm shall not die, neither shall their fire be quenched; and they shall be an abomination unto all flesh.

- OLD: And finding a ship sailing over unto Phenicia, we went aboard, and set forth.
- NEW: And finding a ship sailing over unto Phenicia, we went on board, and set forth.

## abolish → destroy — Isaiah 2:18

- NEW: And the idols he shall utterly destroy.

- NEW: And he did very detestable acts in following idols, according to all things as did the Amorites, whom the Lord cast out before the children of Israel.
